era_context: The Yuan Dynasty in China is in its final years, facing increasing pressure from Ming forces and internal instability. The Hundred Years' War between England and France continues with English forces maintaining significant continental holdings. The Ottoman Empire is consolidating power in Anatolia and beginning to expand into the Balkans. In Europe, the Papacy is undergoing significant institutional changes. This is a period of transition and consolidation across multiple regions.
geographic_coverage_gaps: Central Asian steppes (outside major trade routes)
Southeast Asia (sparse documentation in European sources)

events.title: Gregory XI becomes Pope; Papacy returns to Rome from Avignon
events.description: Pierre Roger de Beaufort is elected Pope and takes the name Gregory XI. Gregory XI is notable for his decision to return the Papacy from Avignon (where it had been based since 1309) to Rome, ending the so-called Avignon Papacy. This return to Rome is a significant symbolic and political act, reasserting papal authority in Italy and the Mediterranean. However, Gregory XI's papacy is marked by health problems and political difficulties, and his return to Rome is not universally welcomed by the French-influenced cardinals.
key_figures: Gregory XI / Pierre Roger de Beaufort (Pope)
events.certainty_note: Gregory XI's election in 1371 is well-documented, but the date given here (1367) appears to be incorrect. Modern scholarship places his election in 1371. This entry may represent a dating error.

events.title: Edward, the Black Prince, defeats Castilian forces at the Battle of Nájera
events.description: Edward, the Black Prince, leads English and allied forces to victory against Castilian forces at the Battle of Nájera during the Castilian Civil War. The Black Prince intervenes in Castilian politics to support Peter of Castile against his rival Henry of Trastámara. The victory is a significant military achievement and demonstrates English military capability in the Iberian Peninsula. However, the political outcome is less favorable, as English intervention fails to secure lasting political advantage in Castile.
key_figures: Peter of Castile (Castilian king supported by English)
events.certainty_note: The Battle of Nájera in 1367 is well-documented in both English and Castilian sources. The date is firmly established in historiography.

events.title: Yuan Dynasty faces increasing military pressure from Ming forces
events.description: The Yuan Dynasty, weakened by internal instability and economic decline, faces increasing military pressure from Ming forces under Zhu Yuanzhang. Ming armies conduct campaigns in northern China, gradually reducing Yuan-controlled territory. This period marks the final phase of Yuan rule, as Ming forces systematically eliminate Yuan strongholds and consolidate control over the Chinese heartland. The military pressure will culminate in the fall of the Yuan capital (Beijing) in 1368.

sources.name: Ming Shilu (Veritable Records of the Ming Dynasty)
Yuan Shih (History of the Yuan Dynasty)

events.certainty_note: The general military situation in 1367 is well-attested, though specific campaign details may vary between sources. The date is approximate within the broader context of Ming military expansion.

events.title: Ottoman forces expand in Anatolia; consolidation of regional power
events.description: Ottoman forces under Murad I continue military campaigns to expand Ottoman control in Anatolia, consolidating the empire's position as a major regional power. The campaigns involve the subjugation of rival Turkish beyliks and the expansion of Ottoman territory at the expense of declining Byzantine and Serbian holdings. This period marks the Ottoman Empire's emergence as a significant military and political force in the Eastern Mediterranean and Balkans.
events.certainty_note: Ottoman military expansion in this period is attested in Ottoman and Byzantine sources, though specific campaign details and dates can vary by ±1–2 years.

events.title: Petrarch completes major literary works; Italian Renaissance intellectual flourishing
events.description: Francesco Petrarch, the Italian poet and humanist, continues his prolific literary output during this period, completing major works that will define Renaissance intellectual culture. Petrarch's writings emphasize classical learning, individual achievement, and the recovery of ancient texts. His work is widely circulated and influential among Italian intellectuals and patrons. This period represents the height of Petrarch's influence and the consolidation of humanist scholarship in Italy.
events.certainty_note: Petrarch's literary activities in 1367 are well-documented through his surviving letters and works. The date is firmly established.
cross_references: 1369-005 (Petrarch's death, though this date is disputed)

disconfirming_evidence: The dating of Gregory XI's election is given as 1367 in this entry, but modern scholarship places it in 1371. This represents a significant dating error. The significance of Ottoman expansion in 1367 is sometimes overstated; the Ottoman Empire's major expansion into the Balkans occurs primarily after 1389. The Battle of Nájera is sometimes portrayed as a more decisive English victory than it actually was; the political outcome was limited.
historiographic_note: Documentation for 1367 is uneven. European sources (particularly English and Italian) are relatively rich, while sources for Asia and Africa are sparser. Chinese sources for the Yuan Dynasty's decline are abundant but reflect the perspective of the victorious Ming Dynasty. Ottoman sources for this period are limited and often compiled centuries later. The dating of Gregory XI's papacy in this entry appears to be incorrect, suggesting that medieval papal chronology requires careful verification. Sub-Saharan African and Pacific history remain largely absent from surviving written records.

graph_edges.note: The Black Prince's Castilian campaign is followed by continued military activities in Aquitaine
Yuan military pressure culminates in the Ming Dynasty founding in 1368
Ottoman expansion in Anatolia sets the stage for Balkan campaigns and the Battle of Kosovo
Petrarch's literary activities are contemporary with the broader Italian Renaissance intellectual flourishing
